enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking
Financial Companies - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial Companies - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: These Directions shall come into effect immediately upon issuance.
enriched_requirements.object: upon issuance

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be applicable to the following Non-Banking Financial
Companies (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'NBFCs' and individually as an
'NBFC'), subject to layer-wise applicability, unless specified otherwise:
Note - The applicability under these Directions is in line with the regulatory
structure for NBFCs as set out in Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial
Companies - Registration, Exemptions and Framework for Scale Based
Regulation) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: following Non-Banking Financial Companies (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'NBFCs' and individually as an 'NBFC')

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation - Securitisation notes issued by the SPE and credit enhancement
facilities available shall be treated as tranches;

(14) 'Tranche maturity' means the tranche's effective maturity in years and is
measured as prescribed in paragraphs 38 to 40;
enriched_requirements.object: in years and is measured as prescribed in paragraphs 38 to 40

conditions:
where D and A are calculated in accordance with
paragraphs 33 to 37
enriched_requirements.object: detachment point (D) minus attachment point (A)

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
and
(16) 'Underlying asset/obligation' means the asset which a protection buyer is seeking
to hedge.
enriched_requirements.object: protection buyer is seeking to hedge

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The terms appearing in paragraphs 19 to 51 on 'Securitisation Exposures' shall bear
the meanings assigned to them under Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial
Companies - Securitisation Transactions) Directions, 2025, unless stated otherwise
herein.
enriched_requirements.object: to them under Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial Companies - Securitisation Transactions) Directions, 2025

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
All other expressions unless defined herein shall have the same meaning as
have been assigned to them under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934 or
Companies Act, 2013 and rules / regulations made thereunder, or any statutory
modification or re-enactment thereto or as used in commercial parlance, as the
case may be.
enriched_requirements.object: as have been assigned to them under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934 or Companies Act, 2013 and rules / regulations made ther

conditions: Provided that paragraphs 6(1)
enriched_requirements.full_sentence: Provided that paragraphs 6(1), 6(2), and 17 shall not apply to an NBFC-MFI.

(2) The provisions contained in paragraphs 6(4), 7, 8, 11, 15, 16, 18(2)(iv), 18(3)
to 18(11), and paragraphs 19 to 54, shall apply to a Housing Finance Company
<image>
(HFC) registered with the RBI under the provisions of the National Housing Bank
Act, 1987, as per layer-wise applicability.
enriched_requirements.object: Finance Company <image> (HFC) registered with the RBI under the provisions of the National Housing Bank Act, 1987, as per layer-wis

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The provisions contained in paragraphs 6(4), 7, 8, 11, 15, 16, and 54 shall
apply to Mortgage Guarantee Company (MGC) registered with RBI under the
Scheme of Registration of Mortgage Guarantee Companies, as per layer-wise
applicability.
enriched_requirements.object: Company (MGC) registered with RBI under the Scheme of Registration of Mortgage Guarantee Companies, as per layer-wise appl

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(4) The provisions contained in paragraph 15, 16, and 54 shall apply to Core
Investment Company (CIC) registered with the RBI under the provisions of the
RBI Act, 1934, as per layer-wise applicability.
enriched_requirements.object: Company (CIC) registered with the RBI under the provisions of the RBI Act, 1934, as per layer-wise applicability

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(5) The provisions contained in paragraph 16 and 54 shall apply to every
Standalone Primary Dealer (SPD) registered with the RBI as an NBFC under the
provisions of the RBI Act, 1934.
enriched_requirements.object: Primary Dealer (SPD) registered with the RBI as an NBFC under the provisions of the RBI Act, 1934

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An NBFC shall meet the following minimum capital requirements on an ongoing
basis:
(1) An NBFC primarily engaged in lending against gold jewellery shall maintain a
minimum Tier 1 capital and Capital to Risk-weighted Asset Ratio (CRAR) of 12
per cent and 15 per cent respectively of the aggregate Risk Weighted Assets
(RWAs).
enriched_requirements.object: capital requirements on an ongoing basis: (1) An NBFC primarily engaged in lending against gold jewellery shall maintain a

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Tier 2 capital of the NBFC, at any point of time, shall not exceed
100 per cent of Tier 1 capital.
enriched_requirements.object: of Tier 1 capital

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation -
CRAR = Tier 1 capital + Tier 2 capital
RWAs
(2) An NBFC - Middle Layer (NBFC - ML) and above shall maintain a minimum Tier
1 capital of 10 per cent of the aggregate RWAs.
enriched_requirements.object: 1 capital of 10 per cent of the aggregate RWAs

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Tier 1 Capital Ratio = Tier 1 capital
Explanation - For paragraph 6(1) and 6(2) above:
RWAs
(3) An NBFC - ML and above shall maintain a minimum CRAR of 15 per cent of the
aggregate RWAs.
enriched_requirements.object: of 15 per cent of the aggregate RWAs

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Tier 2 capital of the NBFC, at any point of time, shall not
exceed 100 per cent of Tier 1 capital.
enriched_requirements.object: of Tier 1 capital

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(4) An NBFC - Upper Layer (NBFC - UL) shall also maintain a minimum Common
Equity Tier 1 (CET1) capital of 9 per cent of the aggregate RWAs.
enriched_requirements.object: Common Equity Tier 1 (CET1) capital of 9 per cent of the aggregate RWAs

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation -
CET1 capital Ratio = CET1 capital
RWAs
(5) An NBFC falling in the Top Layer of the regulatory structure shall, inter alia, be
subject to higher capital charge.
enriched_requirements.object: subject to higher capital charge

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Such higher requirements shall be specifically
communicated to the NBFC at the time of its classification in the Top Layer.
enriched_requirements.object: the NBFC at the time of its classification in the Top Layer

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
'Owned Fund' means aggregate of:
Note - An NBFC shall not be required to deduct an ROU asset (created in terms
of Ind AS 116 - Leases) from owned fund, provided the underlying asset being
taken on lease is a tangible asset.
enriched_requirements.object: an ROU asset (created in terms of Ind AS 116 - Leases) from owned fund

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Tier 1 capital of an NBFC - BL primarily engaged in lending against gold jewellery
and an NBFC - ML shall comprise the following:
Note - An NBFC-BL shall not include PDI in its Tier 1 capital.
enriched_requirements.object: Tier 1 capital

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: Tier 1 capital of an NBFC-UL shall comprise the following:

The PDI shall be issued as bonds or debentures by a non-deposit taking NBFC
on the following terms and conditions to qualify for inclusion as Tier 1 capital or
Tier 2 capital, as the case may be, for capital adequacy purposes:
(1) Currency of issue
PDIs shall be issued in Indian rupees only;
enriched_requirements.object: or debentures by a non-deposit taking NBFC on the following terms and conditions to qualify for inclusion as Tier 1 capital or Tier

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Amount
The aggregate amount to be raised by issue of such instruments shall be within
the overall limits of Tier 1 and Tier 2 as explained in paragraph 12(3) below.
enriched_requirements.object: limits of Tier 1 and Tier 2 as explained in paragraph 12(3) below

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
However, the minimum investment by single investor
in each such issue / tranche shall be ₹5 lakh;

(3) Limits
PDI shall be eligible to be treated as Tier 1 capital up to 15 per cent of total Tier
1 capital as on March 31 of previous year after deduction of goodwill and other
intangible assets but before the deduction of investments.
enriched_requirements.object: treated as Tier 1 capital up to 15 per cent of total Tier 1 capital as on March 31 of previous year after deduction of goodwill and
capital subject to provisions contained in these Directions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(4) Maturity period
The PDI shall be perpetual;

enriched_requirements.object: fixed rate or at a floating rate referenced to a market determined rupee interest benchmark rate

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(6) Options
<image>
A non-deposit taking NBFC shall issue PDI as plain vanilla instruments only.
enriched_requirements.object: vanilla instruments only

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
However, it may issue PDI with a 'call option' subject to strict compliance with
each of the following conditions:
enriched_requirements.object: 'call option' subject to strict compliance with each of the following conditions:

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) call option shall be exercised only with the prior approval of the Reserve
Bank.
enriched_requirements.object: the prior approval of the Reserve Bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
While considering the proposals received from such an NBFC for
exercising the call option, the Reserve Bank shall, among other things, take
into consideration its CRAR position both at the time of exercise of the call
option and after the exercise of the call option;
enriched_requirements.object: take into consideration its CRAR position both at the time of exercise of the call option and after the exercise of the call opt

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(7) Step-up option
The issuing non-deposit taking NBFC may have a step-up option for increasing
the rate of interest payable on PDIs.
enriched_requirements.object: for increasing the rate of interest payable on PDIs

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The step-up shall not be more than 100 bps in reference to interest rate
advertised in terms of offer document under paragraph 12(5) above.
enriched_requirements.object: bps in reference to interest rate advertised in terms of offer document under paragraph 12(5) above

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(i) PDI shall be subject to a lock-in clause in terms of which the issuing non-
deposit taking NBFC shall defer the payment of interest, if:
enriched_requirements.object: lock-in clause in terms of which the issuing non- deposit taking NBFC shall defer the payment of interest

conditions:
when the impact of such payment may result
in net loss or increase the net loss
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) However, a non-deposit taking NBFC may pay interest with the prior
approval of the Reserve Bank when the impact of such payment may result
in net loss or increase the net loss, provided the CRAR remains above the
regulatory norm.
enriched_requirements.object: prior approval of the Reserve Bank when the impact of such payment may result in net loss or increase the net loss

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) The interest shall not be cumulative except in cases as in paragraph 8(i)
above.
enriched_requirements.object: cases as in paragraph 8(i) above
issuing NBFC to the Regional Office of Department of Supervision of the Reserve Bank in whose jurisdiction it is registered

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(9) Seniority of claim
The claims of the investors in PDI shall be:

enriched_requirements.object: progressive discount for capital adequacy purposes since these are perpetual

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(i) PDI shall be fully paid-up, unsecured, free of any restrictive clauses, and
the issue of PDI and the terms and conditions applicable thereto shall be
compliant with the provisions of Companies Act, 2013, and all other laws
for the time being in force including the rules, regulations, directions, and
guidelines issued by the applicable regulatory authorities.
enriched_requirements.object: free of any restrictive clauses, and the issue of PDI and the terms and conditions applicable thereto shall be compliant

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) Subject to compliance with the extant Foreign Exchange Management Act
(FEMA), 1999, an NBFC shall obtain prior approval of the Reserve Bank,
on a case-by-case basis, for investment by Foreign Institutional Investors
(FIIs) in PDI to be raised by a non-deposit taking NBFC in Indian rupees.
enriched_requirements.object: the Reserve Bank, on a case-by-case basis, for investment by Foreign Institutional Investors (FIIs) in PDI to be raised by a
and conditions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(12) Reporting requirements
A non-deposit taking NBFC issuing PDI shall submit a report to the Regional
Office of Department of Supervision of the Reserve Bank under whose
jurisdiction it is registered giving details of the debt raised, including the terms of
issue together with a copy of the offer document soon after the issue is
completed;
enriched_requirements.object: the Regional Office of Department of Supervision of the Reserve Bank under whose jurisdiction it is registered giving details of th

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(13) Investment in PDI issued by other NBFC
<image>
An NBFC investing in PDI issued by other NBFC and financial institutions shall
be subject to the definition of NOF as defined in Section 45-IA of the RBI Act,
1934 and shall attract risk weight as prescribed by the Reserve Bank;
enriched_requirements.object: definition of NOF as defined in Section 45-IA of the RBI Act, 1934 and shall attract risk weight as prescribed by the Reserve Bank
security of its PDI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(i) A non-deposit taking NBFC issuing PDI shall make suitable disclosures in
its Annual Report about:
enriched_requirements.object: its Annual Report about:

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) While framing policy as regards PDI, the Board of Directors of the non-
deposit taking NBFC shall ensure that sufficient disclosures are made to
the investor which clarify the type of the instrument, the risks associated,
and its uninsured nature so as to enable the investor to make informed
investment decision.
enriched_requirements.object: are made to the investor which clarify the type of the instrument, the risks associated, and its uninsured nature s

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The offer document shall contain a clause that
investors shall make investment decision on the basis of their own analysis
and the Reserve Bank does not accept any responsibility about repayment
of such investment.
enriched_requirements.object: investors shall make investment decision on the basis of their own analysis and the Reserve Bank does not accept any responsibil

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The policy evolved by such NBFC shall also include
provision as regards factors to be taken into account by it to demonstrate
that it can meet extra load in case the company decides to step up the rate
of interest under paragraph 12(7) above.
enriched_requirements.object: regards factors to be taken into account by it to demonstrate that it can meet extra load in case the company decides to ste

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Board of Directors shall
ensure strict compliance with all the terms and conditions set forth above.
enriched_requirements.object: all the terms and conditions set forth above

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Tier 2 capital of an NBFC shall comprise the following:
<image>
Note - An NBFC-BL is not eligible to include PDI in its Tier 2 capital.
enriched_requirements.object: Note - An NBFC-BL is not eligible to include PDI in its Tier 2 capital

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that book value of subordinated debt shall be subject to
discounting as provided under:
Remaining Maturity of the instruments Rate of discount
Up to one year 100%
More than one year but up to two years 80%
More than two years but up to three years 60%
More than three years but up to four years 40%
More than four years but up to five years 20%
to the extent such discounted value does not exceed fifty percent of Tier 1
capital;
enriched_requirements.object: as provided under: Remaining Maturity of the instruments Rate of discount Up to one year 100% More than one year but up to t

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The following regulatory treatment shall be provided to DTA and DTL:
(1) DTA shall be treated as an intangible asset by an NBFC;
enriched_requirements.object: and DTL: (1) DTA shall be treated as an intangible asset by an NBFC

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (2) DTA associated with accumulated losses shall be deducted from Tier 1 capital;
enriched_requirements.object: 1 capital

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) DTA (excluding DTA associated with accumulated losses) net of DTL shall be
deducted from Tier 1 capital;
enriched_requirements.object: 1 capital

conditions:
Where DTL is in excess of the DTA (excluding DTA associated with accumulated
losses)
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(4) Where DTL is in excess of the DTA (excluding DTA associated with accumulated
losses), the excess DTL shall neither be adjusted against item (2) nor added to
Tier 1 capital;
enriched_requirements.object: item (2) nor added to Tier 1 capital

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(5) The balance of DTL account shall not be included either in Tier 1 or Tier 2 capital
for capital adequacy purpose;
enriched_requirements.object: Tier 1 or Tier 2 capital for capital adequacy purpose
'others' of 'Other Assets'

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
and
(7) DTL created by debit to opening balance of Revenue Reserves or to Profit and
Loss Account for the current year shall be included under 'others' of 'Other
Liabilities and Provisions'.
enriched_requirements.object: of 'Other Liabilities and Provisions'

conditions:
if an NBFC's contribution is
in the form of subordinated units of any Alternative Investment Fund (AIF)
scheme
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The following adjustments shall be made from regulatory capital:
(1) In terms of Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial Companies -
Undertaking of Financial Services) Directions, 2025, if an NBFC's contribution is
in the form of subordinated units of any Alternative Investment Fund (AIF)
scheme, it shall deduct the entire investment from its capital funds -
proportionately from both Tier 1 and Tier 2 capital (wherever applicable);
enriched_requirements.object: capital: (1) In terms of Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial Companies - Undertaking of Financial Services) Directions, 2025

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) In terms of Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial Companies - Transfer
and Distribution of Credit Risk) Directions, 2025, an NBFC shall adhere to the
applicable Accounting Standards while booking of unrealised profit under Co-
lending Arrangements, if applicable.
enriched_requirements.object: Accounting Standards while booking of unrealised profit under Co- lending Arrangements

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
However, such profits shall be deducted
from CET1 capital or Net Owned Funds for meeting regulatory capital adequacy
requirement till the maturity of such loans.
enriched_requirements.object: capital or Net Owned Funds for meeting regulatory capital adequacy requirement till the maturity of such loans

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Further, for the prudential treatment
of unrealised profits arising because of transfer of loan exposures and Security
Receipts guaranteed by the Government of India, an NBFC shall be guided by
the Directions ibid;
enriched_requirements.object: Directions ibid

conditions:
if an NBFC is the Default Loss Guarantee (DLG)
provider
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
and
(3) In terms of Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial Companies - Credit
Facilities) Directions, 2025, if an NBFC is the Default Loss Guarantee (DLG)
provider, it shall deduct the full amount of DLG, which is outstanding, from its
capital.
enriched_requirements.object: of DLG, which is outstanding, from its capital

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An NBFC, covered by Rule 4 of the Companies (Indian Accounting Standards)
Rules, 2015 [i.e., an NBFC that has implemented Indian Accounting Standards
(Ind AS)], shall be guided by the following while determining 'owned fund', 'CET1
capital', 'Tier 1 capital', and 'regulatory capital':
(1) Any net unrealised gains arising on fair valuation of financial instruments,
including such gains arising on transition to Ind AS, shall not be included in
owned fund and CET1 capital whereas all such net losses shall be considered.
enriched_requirements.object: fund and CET1 capital whereas all such net losses shall be considered

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In determining the net unrealised gains for reduction from owned fund and CET1
capital, the NBFC shall categorise financial assets measured at fair value into
following two categories:
enriched_requirements.object: at fair value into following two categories:
determining CET1 / Tier 1 capital as defined in paragraphs 8 and 10 of these Directions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
While netting may be done within the aforementioned categories, net gains from
one category shall not offset against losses in the other category.
enriched_requirements.object: the other category

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Unrealised
gains / losses shall be considered net of the effect of taxation;
enriched_requirements.object: the effect of taxation

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) As unrealised gains on category (i) in proviso to paragraph 16(1) above have
been excluded in computation of owned fund, an NBFC shall reduce the lower of
acquisition cost or fair value of investments / advances in subsidiaries / other
group companies and other NBFCs while determining Tier 1 capital.
enriched_requirements.object: acquisition cost or fair value of investments / advances in subsidiaries / other group companies and other NBFCs while determining

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Further, net
unrealised gains on category (ii) in proviso to paragraph 16(1) above (i.e.,
'Others'), to the extent they have been excluded in regulatory capital, shall also
be reduced from RWAs;
enriched_requirements.object: RWAs

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) cash flow hedge reserve shall be derecognised while determining owned fund
and CET1 capital;
enriched_requirements.object: owned fund and CET1 capital

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(4) The unrealised gain / loss on a derivative transaction undertaken for hedging
may be offset against the unrealised loss / gain recognised in the capital (either
through Profit or Loss or through Other Comprehensive Income) on the
corresponding underlying hedged instrument.
enriched_requirements.object: unrealised loss / gain recognised in the capital (either through Profit or Loss or through Other Comprehensive Income) on the co

conditions:
If after such offset and netting with
<image>
unrealised gains / losses on other financial instruments
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If after such offset and netting with
<image>
unrealised gains / losses on other financial instruments, there are still net
unrealised gains, the same shall be excluded from regulatory capital.
enriched_requirements.object: capital

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These
unrealised gains / losses shall be considered net of the effect of taxation;
enriched_requirements.object: the effect of taxation

conditions:
Where an NBFC uses fair value as deemed cost at the date of transition with
respect to Property
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(5) Where an NBFC uses fair value as deemed cost at the date of transition with
respect to Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) in terms of Ind AS 101, and the
difference between the deemed cost and the current carrying cost is adjusted
directly in retained earnings, any fair value gains upon such transition shall be
reckoned as Tier 2 capital for an NBFC at a discount of 55 per cent;
enriched_requirements.object: 2 capital for an NBFC at a discount of 55 per cent

conditions: where the credit risk has not increased significantly since initial recognition
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(6) 12 month expected credit loss (ECL) allowances for financial instruments, i.e.,
where the credit risk has not increased significantly since initial recognition, shall
be included under general provisions and loss reserves in Tier 2 capital within
the limits specified by extant regulations.
enriched_requirements.object: provisions and loss reserves in Tier 2 capital within the limits specified by extant regulations

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Lifetime ECL shall not be reckoned for
regulatory capital (numerator) while it shall be reduced from the RWAs;
enriched_requirements.object: capital (numerator) while it shall be reduced from the RWAs

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(7) Securitised assets not qualifying for de-recognition under Ind AS due to credit
enhancement given by an originating NBFC on such assets shall be risk
weighted at zero per cent.
enriched_requirements.object: zero per cent
of the amount of credit enhancement given from Tier 1 capital and the balance from Tier 2 capital

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
and
(8) The balance in the 'Impairment Reserve' shall not be reckoned for regulatory
capital.
enriched_requirements.object: capital

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The leverage ratio of an NBFC - BL shall not be more than seven at any point of
time.
enriched_requirements.object: at any point of time

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: An NBFC shall follow following guidelines for computation of RWAs.
enriched_requirements.object: computation of RWAs

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The value of each on balance
sheet asset / item shall be multiplied with applicable risk weights to arrive at risk-
adjusted value of on balance sheet assets / items.
enriched_requirements.object: risk weights to arrive at risk- adjusted value of on balance sheet assets / items

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The aggregate of risk-adjusted
value of on balance sheet assets shall be taken into account for reckoning the
minimum capital ratio.
enriched_requirements.object: for reckoning the minimum capital ratio

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: Provided that, for sl no.(e)(i) and (e)(ii) above, in the event the projects that qualify as High-quality infrastructure projects subsequently fail to meet these conditions, they shall be subject to risk weights prescribed under Sr.
enriched_requirements.object: weights prescribed under Sr

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
On-balance sheet items Percentage
weight
Provided further that for sl no.(e)(i) and (e)(ii) above the repayment threshold should be determined based on the sanctioned project debt.
enriched_requirements.object: the sanctioned project debt

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: Additional debt, if any, sanctioned as a part of takeover of the loan or otherwise should be clubbed with previous loan(s) sanctioned against the project assets and/or cash flows to determine the repayment threshold.
enriched_requirements.object: loan(s) sanctioned against the project assets and/or cash flows to determine the repayment threshold

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(e) State Government guaranteed claims, which have remained in default for a period of more than 90 days 100
(2) While calculating risk-weighted value of on-balance sheet items following shall
also be considered:

(i) Netting shall be done only in respect of assets where provisions for
depreciation or for bad and doubtful debts have been made.
enriched_requirements.object: respect of assets where provisions for depreciation or for bad and doubtful debts have been made

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) An asset that has been deducted, from owned fund shall have a risk-weight
of 'zero' percentage.
enriched_requirements.object: 'zero' percentage

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) While calculating the aggregate of funded exposure of a borrower for the
purpose of assignment of risk weight, an NBFC shall net off the amount of
cash margin / caution money / security deposits (against which right to set-
off is available) held as collateral against the advances out of the total
outstanding exposure of the borrower.
enriched_requirements.object: of cash margin / caution money / security deposits (against which right to set- off is available) held as collateral against the ad

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) An NBFC may apply zero per cent risk weights in respect of exposures
guaranteed under any existing or future schemes launched by Credit
Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE), Credit
Risk Guarantee Fund Trust for Low Income Housing (CRGFTLIH), and
individual schemes under National Credit Guarantee Trustee Company Ltd
(NCGTC) provided they satisfy the following conditions:
enriched_requirements.object: risk weights in respect of exposures guaranteed under any existing or future schemes launched by Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for M

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(a) Prudential Aspects: The guarantees provided under the respective
schemes shall comply with the requirements for credit risk mitigation,
as applicable for scheduled commercial banks in terms of paragraphs
167 to 176 of the Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks -
Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025, which, inter
alia, requires such guarantees to be direct, explicit, irrevocable and
unconditional.
enriched_requirements.object: for credit risk mitigation, as applicable for scheduled commercial banks in terms of paragraphs 167 to 176 of the Reserve

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(b) Restrictions on permissible claims: Where the terms of the guarantee
schemes restrict the maximum permissible claims through features
like specified extent of guarantee coverage, clause on first loss
absorption by member lending institutions (MLI), payout cap, etc., the
zero percent risk weight shall be restricted to the maximum
permissible claim and the residual exposure shall be subjected to risk
weight as applicable to the counterparty in terms of extant regulations.
enriched_requirements.object: maximum permissible claim and the residual exposure shall be subjected to risk weight as applicable to the counterparty in terms

conditions: In case of a portfolio-level guarantee
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(c) In case of a portfolio-level guarantee, effective from April 1, 2023, the
extent of exposure subjected to first loss absorption by the MLI, if any,
shall be subjected to full capital deduction and the residual exposure
shall be subjected to risk weight as applicable to the counterparty in
terms of extant regulations, on a pro rata basis.
enriched_requirements.object: capital deduction and the residual exposure shall be subjected to risk weight as applicable to the counterparty in terms of extan

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The maximum capital
charge shall be capped at a notional level arrived at by treating the
entire exposure as unguaranteed.
enriched_requirements.object: notional level arrived at by treating the entire exposure as unguaranteed

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(d) Further, subject to the aforementioned prescriptions at paragraph 18
(2)(iv), any future scheme launched under any of the aforementioned
trust funds, in order to be eligible for zero percent risk weight, shall
provide for settlement of the eligible guaranteed claims within thirty
days from the date of lodgement, and the lodgement shall be
permitted within sixty days from the date of default.
enriched_requirements.object: the eligible guaranteed claims within thirty days from the date of lodgement, and the lodgement shall be permitted within si

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: Note - The maximum capital charge shall be capped at a notional level arrived by treating the entire exposure as unguaranteed.
enriched_requirements.object: notional level arrived by treating the entire exposure as unguaranteed

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: 100% of the guaranteed claims shall be paid by the Trust after all avenues for recovery have been exhausted and there is no scope for recovering the default amount.
enriched_requirements.object: Trust after all avenues for recovery have been exhausted and there is no scope for recovering the default amount

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
4.CGTMSE guarantee coverage for Micro- Enterprises
up to ₹5 lakh
Note -
Note - As the counter party exposure shall determine the risk weight, it shall be 100 per cent
in respect of all borrowers or zero per cent if covered by Government guarantee.
enriched_requirements.object: it shall be 100 per cent in respect of all borrowers or zero per cent if covered by Government guarantee

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(12) Commitment to provide liquidity facility for securitisation of standard asset transactions
(13) Second loss credit enhancement for securitisation of standard asset transactions provided by the third party
(14) Other contingent liabilities (To be specified) 50
(5) While calculating risk-weighted value of non-market related off-balance sheet
items following shall also be considered.

An NBFC shall maintain capital against all securitisation exposure amounts,
including those arising from the provision of credit risk mitigants to a
securitisation transaction, investments in asset-backed or mortgage-backed
securities, retention of a subordinated tranche, and extension of a liquidity facility
or credit enhancement.
enriched_requirements.object: securitisation exposure amounts, including those arising from the provision of credit risk mitigants to a securitisation

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For capital computation, whenever securitisation
exposures are a subject of repurchase agreements and repurchased by the
NBFC, the exposure shall be treated as retained exposure and not a fresh
exposure.
enriched_requirements.object: exposure and not a fresh exposure

conditions: where recognised upfront
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An NBFC shall deduct from CET1 or NOF (in case of other regulated
entities which do not have any specific requirement of CET1) any increase in
equity capital resulting from a securitisation transaction, either realised at the
time of sale of underlying assets to the SPE, or unrealised gains on sale of
underlying assets such as that associated with expected future margin income,
where recognised upfront, till the maturity of such assets.
enriched_requirements.object: NOF (in case of other regulated entities which do not have any specific requirement of CET1) any increase in equity capital resulting from a securitisation transaction, either realised at the time of sale of underlying assets to the SPE, or unrealised gains on sale of underlying assets such as that associated with expected future margin income

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For calculating exposure amount, an NBFC shall measure the exposure amount
of its off-balance exposure as follows:
enriched_requirements.object: of its off-balance exposure as follows:

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) for facilities that are not eligible credit risk mitigants, the NBFC shall use a
CCF of 100 per cent;
enriched_requirements.object: 100 per cent

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) for derivatives contracts other than credit risk derivatives contracts, such as
interest rate or currency swaps sold or purchased by the NBFC, to the
extent not covered by paragraphs 20(i) to 20(ii) above, the measurement
approach set out in the paragraph 18(8) shall apply.

For calculating capital requirements, an NBFC's exposure A overlaps another
exposure B if in all circumstances the NBFC shall preclude any loss for the NBFC
on exposure B by fulfilling its obligations with respect to exposure A.
enriched_requirements.object: the NBFC on exposure B by fulfilling its obligations with respect to exposure A

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If an NBFC can
verify that fulfilling its obligations with respect to exposure A shall preclude a loss
<image>
from its exposure to B under any circumstance, the NBFC does not need to
calculate risk-weighted assets for its exposure B.
enriched_requirements.object: from its exposure to B under any circumstance, the NBFC does not need to calculate risk-weighted assets for its exposure B

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
To arrive at an overlap, an NBFC shall, for the purposes of calculating capital
requirements, split or expand its exposures, i.e., splitting exposures into portions
that overlap with another exposure held by the NBFC and other portions that do
not overlap;
enriched_requirements.object: of calculating capital requirements, split or expand its exposures, i

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For example, a liquidity facility shall not be
contractually required to cover defaulted assets in certain circumstances.
enriched_requirements.object: cover defaulted assets in certain circumstances

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For
capital purposes, such a situation shall not be regarded as an overlap to the
securitisation notes issued by that securitisation.
enriched_requirements.object: overlap to the securitisation notes issued by that securitisation

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
However, the NBFC shall
calculate RWAs for the liquidity facility as if it were expanded (either to cover
defaulted assets or in terms of trigger events) to preclude all losses on the
securitisation notes.
enriched_requirements.object: liquidity facility as if it were expanded (either to cover defaulted assets or in terms of trigger events) to preclude all loss

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In such a case, the NBFC shall only need to calculate capital
requirements on the liquidity facility.
enriched_requirements.object: capital requirements on the liquidity facility

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Overlap may also be recognised between relevant capital charges for exposures
in the trading book and capital charges for exposures in the banking book,
provided that the NBFC is able to calculate and compare the capital charges for
the relevant exposures.
enriched_requirements.object: to calculate and compare the capital charges for the relevant exposures

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Liquidity facilities provided by an NBFC that satisfy the requirements of Reserve
Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial Companies - Securitisation Transactions)
Directions, 2025, shall attract risk weights as per the SEC-ERBA approach
prescribed in paragraphs 41 to 47 of these directions.
enriched_requirements.object: per the SEC-ERBA approach prescribed in paragraphs 41 to 47 of these directions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Liquidity facilities provided by an NBFC that do not satisfy the requirements of
Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial Companies - Securitisation
Transactions) Directions, 2025, shall maintain capital charge equal to the actual
exposure, after applying a CCF of 100 per cent for the undrawn portion.
enriched_requirements.object: to the actual exposure, after applying a CCF of 100 per cent for the undrawn portion

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
All securitisation exposures, which are not covered by these directions, or which
do not satisfy the conditions prescribed in these directions (including the
exposures prohibited and conditions prescribed as per Reserve Bank of India
(Non-Banking Financial Companies - Securitisation Transactions) Directions,
2025 or where originator is not a lender referred to in Reserve Bank of India
<image>
(Non-Banking Financial Companies - Securitisation Transactions) Directions,
2025, or for which prudential treatment is not advised explicitly in these directions
or Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial Companies - Securitisation
Transactions) Directions, 2025, a NBFC shall maintain capital charge equal to
the actual exposure and shall be subjected to supervisory scrutiny and suitable
action.
enriched_requirements.object: per Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial Companies - Securitisation Transactions) Directions, 2025 or where origi

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An originator shall maintain capital against the exposures transferred to a SPE,
which then forms the underlying for securitisation notes issued by the SPE, i.e.,
the exposures transferred to a SPE shall be included in the calculation of risk-
weighted assets of the originator and the consideration received from SPE shall
be recognised as an advance, unless the following conditions are satisfied.
enriched_requirements.object: exposures transferred to a SPE, which then forms the underlying for securitisation notes issued by the SPE, i.e., the exposures transferred to a SPE shall be included in the calculation of risk- weighted assets of the originator and the consideration received from SPE shall be recognised as an advance

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation - For this paragraph, retention of servicing rights in respect of the
transferred exposures shall not constitute control by the originator over the
transferred exposures.
enriched_requirements.object: originator over the transferred exposures

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The originator shall not be able to repurchase the transferred exposures unless
it is done through invocation of a clean-up call option.
enriched_requirements.object: the transferred exposures unless it is done through invocation of a clean-up call option

conditions:
Provided that the purchase on invocation of clean-up calls is conducted at arm's
length
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that the purchase on invocation of clean-up calls is conducted at arm's
length, on market terms and conditions (including price / fee) and is subject to
the originator's normal credit approval and review processes.
enriched_requirements.object: of clean-up calls is conducted at arm's length, on market terms and conditions (including price / fee) and is subject to th

conditions: If the originator provides credit enhancement or first loss facility
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(10) If the originator provides credit enhancement or first loss facility, the
securitisation structure shall not allow for increase in the above positions after
inception.
enriched_requirements.object: the above positions after inception

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The originator shall obtain legal opinion that the transfer of exposures to a special
purpose entity satisfies the above conditions if the exposures are to be excluded
from the calculation of RWAs.
enriched_requirements.object: the transfer of exposures to a special purpose entity satisfies the above conditions if the exposures are to be excluded fro

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An NBFC shall apply Securitisation External Ratings Based approach (SEC-
ERBA) for calculation of RWA for credit risk of securitisation exposures.
enriched_requirements.object: Based approach (SEC- ERBA) for calculation of RWA for credit risk of securitisation exposures

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For
unrated securitisation exposures, an NBFC shall maintain capital charge equal
to the actual exposure.
enriched_requirements.object: to the actual exposure

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
However, the originator shall apply a maximum capital requirement for the
securitisation exposures it holds, up to the permissible aggregate threshold,
equal to the capital requirement that shall have been assessed against the entire
underlying loan exposures had they not been securitised.
enriched_requirements.object: requirement for the securitisation exposures it holds, up to the permissible aggregate threshold, equal to the capital require

conditions: When an NBFC provides implicit support to a securitisation
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
When an NBFC provides implicit support to a securitisation, it shall, at a
minimum, hold capital against all the underlying exposures associated with the
securitisation transaction as if they had not been securitised.
enriched_requirements.object: hold capital against all the underlying exposures associated with the securitisation transaction as if they had not been securitised

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Additionally, an
NBFC shall not be permitted to recognise in regulatory capital any gain on sale.
enriched_requirements.object: in regulatory capital any gain on sale

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The attachment point (A) represents the threshold at which losses within the
underlying pool shall first be allocated to the relevant securitisation exposure.
enriched_requirements.object: the relevant securitisation exposure

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
It
shall be expressed as a decimal value between zero and one and shall be equal
to the greater of zero and the ratio of the outstanding balance of the pool of
underlying exposures in the securitisation minus the outstanding balance of all
tranches that rank senior or pari passu to the tranche containing the relevant
securitisation position including the exposure itself to the outstanding balance of
all the underlying exposures in the securitisation.
enriched_requirements.object: decimal value between zero and one and shall be equal to the greater of zero and the ratio of the outstanding balance of the pool of

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
It shall be expressed as a decimal value
between zero and one and shall be equal to the greater of zero and the ratio of
the outstanding balance of the pool of underlying exposures in the securitisation
minus the outstanding balance of all tranches that rank senior to the tranche
containing the relevant securitisation position to the outstanding balance of all
the underlying exposures in the securitisation.
enriched_requirements.object: decimal value between zero and one and shall be equal to the greater of zero and the ratio of the outstanding balance of the pool of

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For the calculation of A and D, over-collateralisation and funded reserve
accounts shall be recognised as tranches;

and the assets forming these reserve
accounts shall be recognised as underlying assets.
enriched_requirements.object: assets

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Only the loss-absorbing part
of the funded reserve accounts that provide credit enhancement shall be
recognised as tranches and underlying assets.
enriched_requirements.object: and underlying assets

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Unfunded reserve accounts, such as those to be funded from future receipts from
the underlying exposures (e.g., unrealised excess spread) and assets that do not
<image>
provide credit enhancement related to these instruments shall not be included in
the above calculation of A and D.
enriched_requirements.object: above calculation of A and D

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An NBFC shall take into consideration the economic substance of the transaction
rather than the form and apply these definitions conservatively in the light of the
structure.
enriched_requirements.object: economic substance of the transaction rather than the form and apply these definitions conservatively in the light of the

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For risk-based capital purposes, tranche maturity (𝑀𝑀𝑇𝑇) shall be measured at the
NBFC's discretion in either of the following manners.
enriched_requirements.object: NBFC's discretion in either of the following manners

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The contractual payments shall be unconditional and shall not be
dependent on the actual performance of the securitised assets.
enriched_requirements.object: actual performance of the securitised assets

conditions:
If such
unconditional contractual payment dates are not available
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If such
unconditional contractual payment dates are not available, the final legal
maturity shall be used.

(MT and ML are in years)
𝑀𝑀𝑇𝑇 = 1 + 0.8(𝑀𝑀𝐿𝐿 − 1)
In all cases, 𝑀𝑀𝑀𝑀 shall have a floor of one year and a cap of five years.
enriched_requirements.object: one year and a cap of five years

conditions: When determining the maturity of a securitisation exposure
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
When determining the maturity of a securitisation exposure, an NBFC shall take
into account the maximum period of time they are exposed to potential losses
from the securitised assets.
enriched_requirements.object: maximum period of time they are exposed to potential losses from the securitised assets

conditions: where an NBFC provides a commitment
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In cases where an NBFC provides a commitment,
the NBFC shall calculate the maturity of the securitisation exposure resulting
from this commitment as the sum of the contractual maturity of the commitment
and the longest maturity of the asset(s) to which the NBFC shall be exposed after
a draw has occurred.
enriched_requirements.object: the securitisation exposure resulting from this commitment as the sum of the contractual maturity of the commitment and the

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For credit protection instruments that are only exposed to losses that occur up to
the maturity of that instrument, an NBFC shall be allowed to apply the contractual
maturity of the instrument and shall not have to look through to the protected
position.
enriched_requirements.object: to the protected position

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For securitisation exposures that are externally rated, RWAs under the SEC-
ERBA shall be determined by multiplying securitisation exposure amounts by the
appropriate risk weights as determined by paragraphs 42 to 44 as mentioned in
these Directions below, provided that the following operational criteria are met:
enriched_requirements.object: securitisation exposure amounts by the appropriate risk weights as determined by paragraphs 42 to 44 as mentioned in these Directions below

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(i) To be eligible for risk-weighting purposes, the external credit assessment
shall take into account and reflect the entire amount of credit risk exposure
the NBFC has with regard to all payments owed to it.
enriched_requirements.object: reflect the entire amount of credit risk exposure the NBFC has with regard to all payments owed to it

conditions:
if a
NBFC is owed both principal and interest
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For example, if a
NBFC is owed both principal and interest, the assessment shall fully take
into account and reflect the credit risk associated with timely repayment of
both principal and interest.
enriched_requirements.object: and reflect the credit risk associated with timely repayment of both principal and interest

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) The external credit assessments shall be from an eligible external credit
rating agency (CRA) as provided in paragraphs 131 to 153 of Reserve Bank
of India (Commercial Banks - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy)
Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: external credit rating agency (CRA) as provided in paragraphs 131 to 153 of Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Prudential N

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A rating shall be published in a publicly accessible form
and included in the CRA's transition matrix.
enriched_requirements.object: publicly accessible form and included in the CRA's transition matrix

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Also, loss and cash flow
analysis as well as sensitivity of ratings to changes in the underlying rating
assumptions shall be publicly available.

Further, the external credit assessment provided by the
eligible CRAs shall not be more than six months old.
enriched_requirements.object: months old

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) Eligible CRAs shall have a demonstrated expertise in assessing
securitisations, which shall be evidenced by strong market acceptance.
enriched_requirements.object: in assessing securitisations, which shall be evidenced by strong market acceptance

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) Furthermore, an NBFC shall not use the credit assessments issued by one
external CRA for one or more tranches and those of another external CRA
for other positions (whether retained or purchased) within the same
securitisation structure that may or may not be rated by the first external
credit rating agency.
enriched_requirements.object: issued by one external CRA for one or more tranches and those of another external CRA for other positions (whether retained

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Where two or more eligible CRAs shall be used and
<image>
these assess the credit risk of the same securitisation exposure differently,
paragraph 151 of Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Prudential
Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025 shall apply.
enriched_requirements.object: these assess the credit risk of the same securitisation exposure differently, paragraph 151 of Reserve Bank of India (Commercial B

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(v) Where CRM is provided to specific underlying exposures or the entire pool
by an eligible guarantor as defined in paragraph 171 of Reserve Bank of
India (Commercial Banks - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy)
Directions, 2025 and is reflected in the external credit assessment assigned
to a securitisation exposure(s), the risk weight associated with that external
credit assessment shall be used.

conditions:
If the CRM provider is not
recognised as an eligible guarantor
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If the CRM provider is not
recognised as an eligible guarantor, the covered securitisation exposures
shall be treated as unrated.

conditions:
where a CRM solely protects a specific securitisation
exposure within a given structure (e.g
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(vi) In the situation where a CRM solely protects a specific securitisation
exposure within a given structure (e.g., asset-backed security tranche) and
this protection is reflected in the external credit assessment, the NBFC shall
treat the exposure as if it is unrated and then apply the CRM treatment
outlined in paragraphs 154 to 181 of Reserve Bank of India (Commercial
Banks - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: if it is unrated and then apply the CRM treatment outlined in paragraphs 154 to 181 of Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks -

conditions:
if an NBFC buys
asset-backed security (ABS) where it provides an unfunded securitisation
exposure (e.g
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For example, if an NBFC buys
asset-backed security (ABS) where it provides an unfunded securitisation
exposure (e.g., liquidity facility or credit enhancement), and that exposure
plays a role in determining the credit assessment on the ABS, the NBFC
shall treat the ABS as if it were not rated.
enriched_requirements.object: if it were not rated

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The NBFC shall continue to hold
capital against the other securitisation exposures it provides (e.g., against
the liquidity facility and / or credit enhancement).
enriched_requirements.object: against the other securitisation exposures it provides (e

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For exposures with short-term ratings, the following risk weights shall apply:
Table: ERBA risk weights for short-term ratings
External credit assessment A1+ / A1 A2 A3 All other ratings
Risk weight 15% 50% 100% 1250%
enriched_requirements.object: weights for short-term ratings External credit assessment A1+ / A1 A2 A3 All other ratings Risk weight 15% 50% 100% 1250%

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Specifically, for exposures with long-term ratings, risk weights shall be
determined according to the following table and shall be adjusted for tranche
maturity and tranche thickness for non-senior tranches as prescribed in
paragraph 45 of these Directions as mentioned below.
enriched_requirements.object: the following table and shall be adjusted for tranche maturity and tranche thickness for non-senior tranches as prescribed

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(i) To account for tranche maturity, an NBFC shall use linear interpolation
between the risk weights for one and five years.
enriched_requirements.object: the risk weights for one and five years

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) To account for tranche thickness, an NBFC shall calculate the risk weight
for non-senior tranches as follows:
𝑅𝑅𝑅𝑅𝑅𝑅𝑅𝑅 𝑤𝑤𝑤𝑤𝑤𝑤𝑤𝑤ℎ𝑡𝑡 = (𝑟𝑟𝑟𝑟𝑟𝑟𝑟𝑟 𝑤𝑤𝑤𝑤𝑤𝑤𝑤𝑤ℎ𝑡𝑡 𝑓𝑓𝑓𝑓𝑓𝑓𝑓𝑓 𝑡𝑡𝑡𝑡𝑡𝑡𝑡𝑡𝑡𝑡 𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎 𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎 𝑓𝑓𝑓𝑓𝑓𝑓 𝑚𝑚𝑚𝑚𝑚𝑚𝑚𝑚𝑚𝑚𝑚𝑚𝑚𝑚𝑚𝑚) ∗
(1 − min (𝑇𝑇, 50%))
where T is the tranche thickness.
enriched_requirements.object: for non-senior tranches as follows: 𝑅𝑅𝑅𝑅𝑅𝑅𝑅𝑅 𝑤𝑤𝑤𝑤𝑤𝑤𝑤𝑤ℎ𝑡𝑡 = (𝑟𝑟𝑟𝑟𝑟𝑟𝑟𝑟 𝑤𝑤𝑤𝑤𝑤𝑤𝑤𝑤ℎ𝑡𝑡 𝑓𝑓𝑓𝑓𝑓𝑓𝑓𝑓 𝑡𝑡𝑡𝑡𝑡𝑡𝑡𝑡𝑡𝑡 𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎 𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎𝑎

conditions: if such an exposure does not exist
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In the case of market risk hedges such as currency or interest rate swaps, the
risk weight shall be inferred from a securitisation exposure that is pari passu to
the swaps or, if such an exposure does not exist, from the next subordinated
tranche.
enriched_requirements.object: securitisation exposure that is pari passu to the swaps or

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In addition,
the resulting risk weight shall never be lower than the risk weight corresponding
to a senior tranche of the same securitisation with the same rating and maturity.
enriched_requirements.object: the risk weight corresponding to a senior tranche of the same securitisation with the same rating and maturity

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For exposures with short-term ratings, the following risk weights shall apply:
Table: ERBA STC risk weights for short-term ratings
External credit assessment A1+ / A1 A2 A3 All other ratings
Risk weight 10% 30% 60% 1250%
enriched_requirements.object: risk weights for short-term ratings External credit assessment A1+ / A1 A2 A3 All other ratings Risk weight 10% 30% 60% 1250%

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For exposures with long-term ratings, risk weights shall be determined according
to the following table and shall be adjusted for tranche maturity, and tranche
thickness for non-senior tranches according to paragraph 44 of these Directions
as mentioned above.
enriched_requirements.object: the following table and shall be adjusted for tranche maturity, and tranche thickness for non-senior tranches according to

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Note - All the criteria mentioned in the Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking
Financial Companies - Securitisation Transactions) Directions, 2025 shall be
satisfied for a securitisation to receive the alternative regulatory capital treatment
as determined by paragraphs 49 to 51 as mentioned above.
enriched_requirements.object: securitisation to receive the alternative regulatory capital treatment as determined by paragraphs 49 to 51 as mentioned above

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: An NBFC shall participate in CDS market as a user.
enriched_requirements.object: as a user

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
As a user, it shall buy credit
protection only to hedge their credit risk on corporate bonds it holds.
enriched_requirements.object: to hedge their credit risk on corporate bonds it holds

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
It shall not
sell protection and hence shall not enter into short positions in the CDS contracts.
enriched_requirements.object: shall not enter into short positions in the CDS contracts

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
It shall exit its bought CDS positions by unwinding them with the original
counterparty or by assigning them in favour of buyer of the underlying bond or
by assigning the contract to any other eligible market participant through novation
(only in case of events such as winding-up or mergers / acquisitions).
enriched_requirements.object: positions by unwinding them with the original counterparty or by assigning them in favour of buyer of the underlying bond or by as

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: An NBFC, as user of CDS, shall fulfil the following requirements regarding CDS.
enriched_requirements.object: regarding CDS

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(1) Operational requirements for CDS
Note - The maturity of the underlying exposure and the maturity of the
hedge shall both be defined conservatively.

The effective maturity of the
underlying shall be gauged as the longest possible remaining time before
the counterparty is scheduled to fulfill its obligation, taking into account any
applicable grace period.
enriched_requirements.object: longest possible remaining time before the counterparty is scheduled to fulfill its obligation, taking into account any applicable gr

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(i) A CDS contract shall represent a direct claim on the protection seller and
shall be explicitly referenced to specific exposure, so that the extent of the
cover is clearly defined and incontrovertible.
enriched_requirements.object: on the protection seller and shall be explicitly referenced to specific exposure, so that the extent of the cover is clearly

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) Other than non-payment by a protection buyer of premium in respect of the
credit protection contract, it shall be irrevocable.

(iii) There shall be no clause in the contract that shall allow the protection seller
unilaterally to cancel the credit cover or that would increase the effective
cost of cover as a result of deteriorating credit quality in the hedged
exposure.
enriched_requirements.object: the contract that shall allow the protection seller unilaterally to cancel the credit cover or that would increase the effective cost

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (iv) The CDS contract shall be unconditional.

There shall be no clause in the
protection contract outside the direct control of the NBFC that could prevent
the protection seller from being obliged to pay out in a timely manner in the
event that the original counterparty fails to make the payment(s) due.
enriched_requirements.object: the protection contract outside the direct control of the NBFC that could prevent the protection seller from being obliged to pay out

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(v) The credit events specified by the contracting parties shall at a minimum
cover:

conditions:
when the restructuring of the underlying obligation is not covered by
the CDS
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(d) when the restructuring of the underlying obligation is not covered by
the CDS, but the other requirements in paragraph 53(1) are met,
partial recognition of the CDS shall be allowed.

conditions:
If the CDS specifies deliverable obligations that are different from the
underlying obligation
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(vi) If the CDS specifies deliverable obligations that are different from the
underlying obligation, the resultant asset mismatch shall be governed under
paragraph 53(1)(x).

(vii) The CDS shall not terminate prior to expiration of any grace period required
for a default on the underlying obligation to occur as a result of a failure to
pay.
enriched_requirements.object: of any grace period required for a default on the underlying obligation to occur as a result of a failure to pay

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An NBFC shall make a thorough internal assessment of the need for capital,
commensurate with the risks in its business.
enriched_requirements.object: assessment of the need for capital, commensurate with the risks in its business

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
This internal assessment shall be
on similar lines as ICAAP prescribed for commercial banks under Pillar 2 under
Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Prudential Norms on Capital
Adequacy) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: as ICAAP prescribed for commercial banks under Pillar 2 under Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Prudential Norms on Capita

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
While Pillar 2 capital will not be insisted upon, an
NBFC shall make a realistic assessment of risks.
enriched_requirements.object: of risks

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Internal capital assessment
shall factor in credit risk, market risk, operational risk, and all other residual risks
as per methodology to be determined internally.
enriched_requirements.object: market risk, operational risk, and all other residual risks as per methodology to be determined internally

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The methodology for internal
assessment of capital shall be proportionate to the scale and complexity of
operations as per its Board approved policy.
enriched_requirements.object: scale and complexity of operations as per its Board approved policy

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The
Directions, instructions and guidelines repealed prior to the issuance of these
Directions shall continue to remain repealed.

Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken,
or initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall
continue to be governed by the provisions thereof.
enriched_requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or
acknowledgments granted under these repealed lists shall be deemed as
governed by these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: by these Directions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Further, the repeal of these directions,
instructions, or guidelines shall not in any way prejudicially affect:
enriched_requirements.object: affect:

conditions: if those directions
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
and any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may be instituted,
continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may
be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guidelines had not been
repealed.
enriched_requirements.object: enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guideline

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation
of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations or directions, for the time
being in force.
enriched_requirements.object: and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations or directions, for the time being in force

conditions: if it considers necessary
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order
to remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of
these Directions, the Reserve Bank̥ may, if it considers necessary, issue
necessary clarifications in respect of any matter covered herein and the
interpretation of any provision of these Directions given by the Reserve Bank
shall be final and binding.
